delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
A 15-week English Conversation/Communication Course with a speaking midterm & final exam at the end.
Students will have weekly themes/topics to prepare, read, write, think and talk about in class and as homework.
This class should be fun and easy to participate in as you improve your English conversation/communication ability discussing weekly topics in groups.
This is your chance to show us what you know about your culture and learn more about other cultures.
aims:
English Conversation I is a basic introductory course to university English communication taught by a native English speaker.
The aim is that students improve general communicative competence and learn to apply this productively and receptively in authentic spoken interactions.
This is an opportunity to communicate in groups using the English you have learned in junior and senior high school.
Have fun improving your English conversation/communication ability discussing weekly topics about everyday activities as well worldwide events and cultures.
goals: Speaking and listening in English to develop basic conversational communication ability
Reading, writing and speaking in English to develop practical grammar ability
Speaking in pairs or small groups to improve English pronunciation and fluency
Reading, writing and speaking about customs and culture to improve intercultural understanding

plan.text:
Introduction/ Class Orientation
English Conversation Handbook
Written/ Spoken Self-introduction

Self-introductions/ Making & Asking Questions
Give your self-introduction to the class
Write & ask 8~10 questions (WH-?s)
Do self-assessment

Family & Hometowns
Written/ Spoken Family/ Hometown
Write at least 10 sentences about your family & hometown
Practice talking in small groups
SDGs sustainable cities & communities

Hometowns : Listening & Pronunciation Practice
Listen to other students & online KU moodle recordings
Pronunciation practice of words/phrases

Sports: Popular Hobbies & the Olympics
Write & practice speaking about your favorite sports & hobbies
SDGs gender equality/reduced inequalities

Mirroring, Shadowing , & Making Comments
Pronunciation practice by listening first,
then practice repeating (mirroring/shadowing) words & phrases,
& finally practice making comments ( Really/ Wow/ I see...... )

Entertainment : Movies/TV Shows/Netflix/YouTube/Social Media
Write & practice speaking about the kinds of entertainment you like/dislike
What's/ Who's your favorite .........? Why?
SDGs peace, justice, strong institutions

Preparation & MID-TERM ORAL EXAM (group conversation) [ 20% ]
MID-TERM EXAM [20%]: Ability to keep conversation going
Good content/ information
Good pronunciation
Good sentences & grammar
Practice speaking, asking questions, & commenting naturally in a group conversation
Talk about the topics (family, hometowns, sports, entertainment) we have studied in class.

Food/Restaurants: Japanese & Ethnic
Practice writing & speaking about your favorite foods & restaurants
SDGs zero hunger/good health & well-being/clean water

Making Follow-up Questions / Restaurants
Practice listening to & having conversations
Write one or two questions to ask to keep the conversation going
SDGs responsible consumption & production

Culture & Customs : Local/ International
Practice writing & talking about a Japanese or local customs
SDGs peace, justice, strong institutions/partnership to achieve goals

Shopping : Money & Numbers
Practice writing & talking about where & how you shop, & what you like to buy
SDGs responsible consumption & production

Travel: Vacations & School Trips
Practice writing & talking about a vacation you have taken or your future dream vacation
SDGs quality education/responsible consumption & production

Current Events /Trends/Review
Practice writing & talking about events in the news (worldwide/locally) or around you
SDGs peace, justice, strong institutions/climate action

Prepare/Do FINAL ORAL EXAM (group CONVERSATION) [20%]
Practice speaking, asking questions, & commenting naturally in a group conversation
Talk about the topics (food/restaurants, customs/culture, shopping,travel, current events)

FINAL ORAL EXAM (group CONVERSATION) [20%]
Ability to keep conversation going/asking questions/commenting
Good content/asking questions about topic
Good pronunciation/mirroring & shadowing
Good sentences & grammar using words & phrases used in class

textbooks:
Original materials and handouts, along with videos will be used in class.
You can look at the class moodle website [https://moodle.kochi-u.ac.jp/2026/] to check on weekly topics & assignments.
All students will be given the Eikaiwa Handbook reference in Eikaiwa I class.
prereq: This course is a requirement for all first-year students using the basic English knowledge you acquired in junior and senior high school.
prep:
Students are expected to practice English skills and prepare for all classes.
Practice outside of the classroom is essential.
At least 30~45 minutes are needed weekly to review, practice & prepare for each class at home.
To improve your English ability to a higher level, 3~4 hours weekly of study at home is recommended.
Try practicing English at home or outside of class for at least 5~10 minutes daily to make an improvement.
teachers: ◎ フェビアン ポーラ ダイアン

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: 【テーマ（英語）】(IN ENGLISH)
extra.text:
Enjoy communicating in English.
Give students an opportunity to communicate in groups in English.
Keep a conversation/discussion going using weekly topics as well as good pronunciation & grammar.

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
人類の文化・社会・自然に関する知識
課題探求力
語学に関するリテラシー
情報に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力
自律力

extra.label:
履修に係わる注意事項
【NOTES ON CLASS ENROLLMENT】
extra.text:
It is very important to ATTEND class regularly (maximum 3 absences), especially the 1st lesson introduction.
Students are expected to ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE in every class.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
60% In-class work: Group discussion/Effort/Participation
Preparation: Homework/Vocabulary Notebook or folder/(Moodle)
(MAXIMUM ***3 absences)
40% EXAMS: 20% Midterm EXAM (Group CONVERSATION)
20% Final EXAM (Group CONVERSATION)

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連

extra.label: 授業形態
extra.text:
口頭での質疑やディスカッションを含む
授業中や授業時間外のグループワークを含む
